title: Saag aloo
subtitle: Spinach and potato dry curry
excerpt: Potato cubes cooked with wilted spinach, cumin, turmeric, garlic, and ginger in a dry curry with no sauce. The spinach is added in large quantities and reduces down dramatically, coating the potatoes in a dark green, fragrant paste. Served as a side dish at the British curry-house table.
sourceNotes: Saag aloo (spinach and potato) is a Punjabi dry curry standard, widely documented in British-Indian cookbooks from the 1970s onward. 'Saag' in the British curry-house context refers to spinach; in Punjabi cooking the term more broadly covers leafy greens including mustard leaves.

content.text: Spinach reduces to a fraction of its raw volume when wilted. A full 400 g of spinach becomes a thin, concentrated layer once the moisture has cooked off. Add it in batches, letting each addition wilt before adding the next, and then cook off the liquid until the dish is dry. This concentrated spinach coating is what makes the dish different from spinach-in-sauce preparations.

The potatoes need to be fully cooked before the spinach is added, otherwise the spinach overcooks while waiting for the potato to catch up. Par-cook the cubed potato in salted boiling water for 7 to 8 minutes until just tender, then drain and add to the spiced oil to finish.
Par-cook the potato and build the base

Boil the {{potato}} cubes in salted water for 7 to 8 minutes until just tender. Drain and set aside. Heat the {{vegetable-oil}} in a wide pan over medium-high heat. Add the {{mustard-seeds-black}} and {{cumin-seeds}} and cook for 30 seconds until they pop. Add the {{onion}} and cook for 8 minutes until golden. Add the {{garlic}}, {{ginger-root}}, {{turmeric}}, and {{chilli-powder}} and cook for 1 minute. Add the potatoes and stir to coat in the spices.

Add the {{spinach}} in three or four batches, letting each batch wilt before adding the next. Once all the spinach has wilted, increase the heat and cook for 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until most of the liquid has evaporated and the spinach coats the potatoes. Season with salt and stir in the {{garam-masala}} to finish.

items.cause: Spinach liquid not reduced off or potatoes wet from boiling
items.fix: Drain potatoes thoroughly and pat dry. After the spinach wilts, cook on high heat with the lid off to reduce the liquid

items.symptom: Spinach has turned brown and dull
items.cause: Cooked too long at high heat
items.fix: Reduce the heat once the spinach has wilted. It should be dark green and glossy, not khaki and dry

items.cause: Over-par-cooked before adding to the pan
items.fix: Par-cook to just tender — they should hold their shape. They will soften a little more when added to the pan
Spinach absorbs salt. Taste and season generously. The turmeric and cumin should be perceptible

content.text: Saag aloo is the standard vegetarian side dish on the British curry-house menu, ordered alongside rice and a main curry. It is a dish that travels well between restaurant and home kitchen: the technique is simple, the ingredients are always available, and the result is reliable. The combination of potato and leafy greens is one of the foundational pairings of North Indian and Pakistani cooking, appearing across different seasons and regions with different greens, different potatoes, and different spice combinations.
